mission.title: Install and Configure Ovn Kubernetes on Kubernetes
mission.description: OVN-Kubernetes is a robust networking solution for Kubernetes clusters that leverages Open Virtual Networking (OVN) and Open vSwitch (OVS). Installing OVN-Kubernetes enables advanced networking capabilities for your Kubernetes applications.

steps.title: Add the OVN-Kubernetes Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official OVN-Kubernetes Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add ovn-kubernetes https://github.com/ovn-org/ovn-kubernetes/releases/download/v1.2.0
```

steps.title: Install OVN-Kubernetes using Helm
steps.description:
Install the OVN-Kubernetes Helm chart in a new namespace called 'ovn-kubernetes' with the following command:
```bash
helm install ovn-kubernetes ovn-kubernetes/ovn-kubernetes --namespace ovn-kubernetes --create-namespace --version 1.2.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that all OVN-Kubernetes pods are running by executing:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace ovn-kubernetes
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits for OVN-Kubernetes
steps.description:
To ensure optimal performance, you can set resource limits for the OVN-Kubernetes components. Edit the values file or use the following command to set limits:
```bash
helm upgrade ovn-kubernetes ovn-kubernetes/ovn-kubernetes --namespace ovn-kubernetes --set resources.limits.cpu=500m --set resources.limits.memory=512Mi
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of OVN-Kubernetes will show all pods in the 'ovn-kubernetes' namespace as running. You can also verify the resource limits have been applied by checking the pod specifications.
codeSnippets: helm upgrade ovn-kubernetes ovn-kubernetes/ovn-kubernetes --namespace ovn-kubernetes --set resources.limits.cpu=500m --set resources.limits.memory=512Mi

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, and have Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
